name: Three Tables
tagline: The easiest reef entry on the North Shore — walk right in from sand and float over a turtle-dotted reef.
parking: Shares free lot with Shark's Cove across the street
hours: Open 24 hours (no gates; Pupukea Beach Park)
bestTime: Summer (May–Sep) for calm conditions; morning for best visibility
talkStory: Named for three flat reef platforms that emerge at low tide, this site sits within the Pupukea Marine Life Conservation District where fishing and collecting are prohibited — so reef life is noticeably denser than unprotected areas. The sandy channel between the tables is a cleaning station where honu line up to let cleaner wrasse pick parasites off their shells. Winter swells turn this same reef into a violent washing machine — the season shift catches visitors off guard.
